svg.title: The layers of Corvin (layers 0–5 + MCP Plugin Manager)
svg.desc: Seven horizontal layers: Claude Code (layer 0), voice (1), bridges (2), cowork (3), auto-routing (4), forge with policy and audit (5), and MCP Plugin Manager (ADR-0096). Each layer carries one-line descriptions of what it owns and what it does not own.
svg.text: The layers of Corvin (0–5 + MCP Plugin Manager)
Each layer is independently disable-able; layers below keep working when one above is removed.
MCP Plugin Manager (ADR-0096) — user-installable external MCP servers
Owns: catalog.json, active.json (user+tenant), per-session + per-project scope files, builtin manifests. L10+L16+L34+L35 enforced.
Layer 5 · forge — runtime tool factory + policy + audit
Owns: register / call / audit forged tools. Self-contained, splittable. Bridge audit chains in via shared/audit.py.
Layer 4 · auto-routing
Owns: pick a persona per message. Heuristic + embedding + fallback. Does not run any tool — it labels.
Layer 3 · cowork — personas
Owns: per-chat tool surface, system prompt, MCP servers. mcp_plugins_allowed restricts catalog injection. Enforcement in adapter.
Layer 2 · bridges
Owns: WhatsApp / Telegram / Discord / Slack / Email / Teams / Signal daemons + adapter loop. Whitelist is the trust boundary.
Layer 1 · voice
Owns: read-aloud at the desk, voice-note transcription via Whisper, summarization policy. Does not pick personas.
Layer 0 · Claude Code (vanilla, unchanged)
Owns: agent loop, tool dispatcher, LLM client, sub-agents. Surfaces consumed: plugins, hooks, MCP servers.
splittable
optional
optional
required
Yellow = MCP Plugin Manager (ADR-0096). Cyan = forge (ADR-0011). Blue = inherited stack. Slate = vanilla Claude Code.
